findings.summary: Legacy KiCad 5 .sch files (daisho: 60 files, OpenVent: 19 files) have 0 signal detections. Component extraction and net connectivity work, subcircuit detection works (231 subcircuits in daisho, 98 in OpenVent), but signal pattern analysis not wired up for legacy format.

correct.description: All legacy components correctly extracted with reference, value, lib_id
231 subcircuits detected in daisho, demonstrating IC+neighbor grouping works

suggestions: Wire up run_signal_analysis() in parse_legacy_schematic() return path — the net data and component data exist, just need to pass them through the signal detectors

findings.summary: Legacy power.sch has 160 nets but only 4 with multiple pins. Most components appear isolated (single-pin nets). Wire-to-pin matching may have gaps in complex legacy sheets.

correct.description: GND net correctly has 10 pins, VUSB has 3 — named nets work

suggestions: Investigate legacy wire-to-pin matching tolerance. KiCad 5 uses mils coordinates; ensure endpoint matching handles coordinate precision correctly.

findings.summary: X1/X2/X3 (40/12/25 MHz crystals, QUARTZCMS4_GROUND lib) classified as "connector" instead of "crystal"

incorrect.description: 3 crystals with ref prefix X misclassified as connectors. Library symbol is QUARTZCMS4_GROUND.

suggestions: Add X prefix to crystal classification, or check library name for crystal/quartz keywords
